title: thymus funkii subsp. sabulicola
intro: Thymus funkii subsp. sabulicola, also known as the Sand Thyme or Coastal Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus funkii plant, which is a low-growing, shrubby perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils. The health benefits of Thymus funkii subsp. sabulicola include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues and skin conditions. Thymus funkii subsp. sabulicola can be prepared for medicinal use in various ways, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
